context: User has ~/.claude.json with mcpServers configured: 'linear-perdix', 'linear-sonner', 'notion', 'context7', 'github', 'shadcn', 'trigger'. Two Linear workspaces are intentional (multi-workspace setup). Some entries have only a 'command' field; others have full env blocks.
expected_behavior: Output is valid JSON with four array-valued keys: linear, notion, github, other
linear array contains BOTH 'linear-perdix' and 'linear-sonner' (substring or prefix match on the name)
other array contains the remaining names (context7, shadcn, trigger) — exact spelling preserved
Order within each array is stable across runs (deterministic — alphabetical or insertion order, but consistent)
Missing ~/.claude.json (or empty mcpServers) returns all four arrays as empty, exit 0 — NOT exit 2
Invalid JSON in the config file exits 2 with stderr error message
--config <PATH> overrides the default location and reads that file instead
Errors go to stderr; stdout is always parseable JSON or empty
failure_modes: Returns only one of the two Linear entries — fails to detect multi-workspace setup, breaks claim's workspace-picker AskUserQuestion
Groups 'linear-perdix' under 'other' because the name doesn't match exactly 'linear'
Exits 2 when ~/.claude.json is missing instead of returning empty arrays
Crashes on entries without a full env block — assumes shape that not all MCPs have
Logs to stdout, polluting JSON output
Order changes across runs (depends on dict iteration order), making downstream caller logic flaky
